expectations.text: The skill reads the original article before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Summary references Section 3.4 content from RC-BL-02 ('Square Brackets [ ] and Their Uses', line 115 reference table)

expectations.text: The skill identifies that no correction is needed (article already correct)
expectations.evidence: Summary states: 'The article already contains the CORRECT checkbox syntax format' and 'STATUS: No correction was needed'

expectations.text: No unnecessary changes are made to the article
expectations.evidence: Summary states 'File copied to outputs/ for reference without modifying the original kb/ directory' — output is a clean copy, no edits

expectations.text: The skill communicates clearly why no edit was made
expectations.evidence: Summary explains the correct format found, where in the article it is, and why no change was needed — user would understand

expectations.text: The Q&A is added to RC-ALERT-01 (Setup article, not Management article)
expectations.evidence: Output file is RC-ALERT-01_Alerts-and-Notifications-Setup.md; summary confirms 'Article Modified: RC-ALERT-01'

expectations.text: The added question is phrased as a real user would ask it (not as a policy statement)
expectations.evidence: Question reads: 'Do alerts fire when a project is in draft or development status?' — natural user phrasing

expectations.text: The answer is direct and complete — explains the behavior and why
expectations.evidence: Answer explains production status requirement and that draft mode prevents accidental sends during setup/testing

expectations.text: The article metadata version number is incremented
expectations.evidence: Version field in output file is 1.1 (original was 1.0)

expectations.text: The skill notes whether KB-REFERENCE-MAP needs updating
expectations.evidence: Summary explicitly states 'No cross-reference changes needed (KB-REFERENCE-MAP.md not updated as no new article references were added)'

expectations.text: No new article is created without user confirmation
expectations.evidence: No new .md article files in outputs/ directory; response.txt only contains assessment and question for user

expectations.text: The skill reads KB-REFERENCE-MAP or existing SURV articles before deciding
expectations.evidence: Response references RC-SURV-07 section details (Section 3, 4.4, 9, 10) showing the article was read, and identifies RC-SURV-03 as alternative target

expectations.text: The routing decision is specific — names which article(s) the content belongs in
expectations.evidence: Routes to RC-SURV-07 Section 10 (gotcha) and RC-SURV-03 (auto-continue), with section-level specificity

expectations.evidence: Response has a 'QUESTION FOR USER' section with 3 specific clarifying questions before proceeding
